courses.name: High school chemistry
courses.subject: chemistry
units: States of matter and intermolecular forces

concepts.title: Atomic structure: protons, neutrons, and electrons
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: The nucleus of protons and neutrons surrounded by electrons, and how subatomic particle counts define an atom's identity and charge.

concepts.title: Isotopes and average atomic mass
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Atoms of the same element with different neutron counts, and how isotopic abundances combine into the weighted average atomic mass on the periodic table.

concepts.title: Ion formation and charge
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: How atoms gain or lose electrons to form cations and anions, and the relationship between electron transfer and net charge.

concepts.title: Electron configuration
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: How electrons fill orbitals and energy levels following the Aufbau principle, Pauli exclusion, and Hund's rule, written as configuration notation.

concepts.title: Atomic orbitals and the quantum mechanical model
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: The shapes and orientations of s, p, and d orbitals as probability regions for electrons in the quantum mechanical model of the atom.

concepts.title: Periodic trends
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: How atomic radius, ionization energy, electron affinity, and electronegativity vary across periods and down groups of the periodic table.

concepts.title: Ionic bonding and lattices
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions and how it builds repeating crystal lattices in ionic solids.

concepts.title: Covalent bonding and Lewis structures
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Sharing of electron pairs between atoms and how to draw Lewis dot structures showing bonding and lone pairs to satisfy the octet rule.

concepts.title: VSEPR theory and molecular geometry
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: How electron-pair repulsion sets the 3D shape of a molecule (linear, trigonal, tetrahedral, bent) and the bond angles that result.

concepts.title: Bond polarity and molecular polarity
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: How electronegativity differences create polar bonds and how bond dipoles combine with geometry to make a molecule polar or nonpolar.

concepts.title: Naming ionic compounds and writing formulas
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Rules for naming ionic compounds and polyatomic ions and balancing charges to write correct chemical formulas.

concepts.title: Naming covalent compounds
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Using Greek prefixes to name molecular compounds formed from two nonmetals and translating names back into formulas.

concepts.title: Balancing chemical equations
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Adjusting coefficients so atoms are conserved on both sides of a chemical equation, enforcing the law of conservation of mass.

concepts.title: Types of chemical reactions
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Classifying reactions as synthesis, decomposition, single replacement, double replacement, or combustion based on their patterns.

concepts.title: The mole and Avogadro's number
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: The mole as a counting unit linking the number of particles to mass via Avogadro's number and molar mass.

concepts.title: Stoichiometry and limiting reactants
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Using mole ratios from a balanced equation to compute product amounts and identify the limiting reactant and percent yield.

concepts.title: Intermolecular forces
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.unit: States of matter and intermolecular forces
concepts.description: London dispersion, dipole-dipole, and hydrogen bonding between molecules and how their strength governs boiling point and state.

concepts.title: States of matter and phase changes
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.unit: States of matter and intermolecular forces
concepts.description: Particle arrangement in solids, liquids, and gases, and the energy changes during melting, boiling, and sublimation shown on a heating curve.

concepts.title: Gas laws and the ideal gas law
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Relationships among pressure, volume, temperature, and moles (Boyle, Charles, Gay-Lussac) unified by the ideal gas law PV = nRT.

concepts.title: Kinetic molecular theory of gases
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: The model of gases as fast-moving particles in constant random motion, explaining pressure and temperature in terms of molecular kinetic energy.

concepts.title: Solutions, solubility, and concentration
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: How solutes dissolve in solvents, factors affecting solubility, and expressing concentration as molarity.

concepts.title: Energy changes and enthalpy in reactions
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Exothermic and endothermic reactions, enthalpy change, and reading energy diagrams that show reactant and product energy levels.

concepts.title: Chemical equilibrium and Le Chatelier's principle
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Dynamic equilibrium in reversible reactions and how changing concentration, pressure, or temperature shifts the balance per Le Chatelier's principle.

concepts.title: Acids, bases, and the pH scale
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Defining acids and bases, the pH scale, and how hydrogen-ion concentration determines acidity and basicity.

concepts.title: Radioactive decay and nuclear reactions
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Alpha, beta, and gamma decay, half-life, and the difference between fission and fusion in nuclear reactions.

concepts.title: Photoelectron spectroscopy (PES)
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Reading PES spectra to infer electron configuration and subshell energies from peak positions and relative heights. AP-specific depth beyond HS atomic structure.

concepts.title: Coulomb's law and periodic trends
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Quantitative explanation of atomic radius and ionization energy trends using Coulomb's law, effective nuclear charge, and shielding. AP-specific depth on HS periodic trends.

concepts.title: Mass spectrometry of elements
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Interpreting mass spectra to determine isotopic masses and abundances and compute average atomic mass.

concepts.title: Elemental composition and empirical formulas
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Determining empirical and molecular formulas from percent composition and combustion analysis data.

concepts.title: Hybridization and sigma/pi bonds
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Mixing of atomic orbitals into sp, sp2, and sp3 hybrids and how sigma and pi bonds account for molecular geometry and multiple bonds. AP-specific extension of HS VSEPR/bonding.

concepts.title: Resonance and formal charge
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Drawing resonance structures, assigning formal charges, and using them to select the most stable Lewis representation.

concepts.title: Metallic bonding and alloys
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: The electron-sea model of metallic bonding and how interstitial and substitutional alloys explain metal properties.

concepts.title: Types of solids and crystal lattices
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Classifying ionic, molecular, covalent network, and metallic solids and relating their lattice structure to physical properties.

concepts.title: Solutions, mixtures, and separation techniques
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Particle-level view of solutions and physical separation methods such as chromatography and distillation based on intermolecular interactions.

concepts.title: Beer-Lambert law and spectroscopy
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: How absorbance relates linearly to concentration and path length, enabling concentration measurement via UV-Vis spectroscopy.

concepts.title: Real gases and deviations from ideality
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Why real gases deviate from the ideal gas law at high pressure and low temperature due to molecular volume and attractions.

concepts.title: Net ionic equations and precipitation
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Writing complete and net ionic equations for precipitation reactions by removing spectator ions.

concepts.title: Oxidation-reduction reactions
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Assigning oxidation numbers, identifying oxidation and reduction half-reactions, and balancing redox equations.

concepts.title: Stoichiometry and titrations
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Using titration data and solution stoichiometry to determine unknown concentrations at the equivalence point.

concepts.title: Reaction rates and rate laws
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Defining reaction rate and determining rate-law order and rate constants from concentration-versus-time data.

concepts.title: Integrated rate laws and half-life
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Using integrated rate laws for zero, first, and second order reactions to find concentration over time and half-life via linearized plots.

concepts.title: Collision theory and activation energy
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: How molecular collisions, orientation, and the activation-energy barrier (Arrhenius equation) control reaction rate and temperature dependence.

concepts.title: Reaction mechanisms and catalysis
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Elementary steps, the rate-determining step, intermediates, and how catalysts lower activation energy by providing an alternate pathway.

concepts.title: Enthalpy, calorimetry, and Hess's law
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Measuring heat with calorimetry and computing reaction enthalpy from bond energies, formation enthalpies, and Hess's law. AP-specific depth on HS thermochemistry.

concepts.title: Entropy and Gibbs free energy
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: How entropy change and Gibbs free energy (G = H - TS) determine whether a process is thermodynamically favorable.

concepts.title: Equilibrium constant and the reaction quotient
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Writing K expressions, comparing Q to K to predict direction, and solving for equilibrium concentrations with ICE tables. AP-specific depth on HS equilibrium.

concepts.title: Solubility equilibria and Ksp
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Using the solubility product constant Ksp to predict precipitation and the common-ion effect on solubility.

concepts.title: Weak acid-base equilibria, Ka and Kb
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Calculating pH of weak acid and base solutions using Ka, Kb, and percent ionization. AP-specific depth on HS acid-base.

concepts.title: Buffers and titration curves
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: How buffers resist pH change (Henderson-Hasselbalch) and interpreting acid-base titration curves and equivalence points.

concepts.title: Galvanic and electrolytic cells
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Cell construction, electrode reactions, and computing cell potential and its link to free energy; electrolysis as the driven reverse process.

concepts.title: Free energy, cell potential, and the Nernst equation
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Linking standard cell potential, Gibbs free energy, and the equilibrium constant, and using the Nernst equation for nonstandard conditions.

concepts.title: Atomic orbitals and hybridization in organic molecules
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: How sp, sp2, and sp3 hybridization of carbon determines bond angles, sigma/pi bonding, and the 3D shape of organic molecules.

concepts.title: Bond-line structures and condensed formulas
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Reading and drawing skeletal (bond-line) structures, condensed formulas, and converting between organic structural representations.

concepts.title: Electronegativity, bond polarity, and inductive effects
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: How electronegativity differences create dipoles and inductive effects that shape reactivity and acidity in organic molecules.

concepts.title: Resonance structures and delocalization
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Drawing resonance structures, using curved arrows to move electrons, and ranking contributors to understand delocalized systems.

concepts.title: Organic acids and bases
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Predicting relative acidity and basicity from conjugate-base stability, resonance, induction, and hybridization (the ARIO factors).

concepts.title: IUPAC nomenclature and functional groups
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Systematic IUPAC naming of alkanes and substituents and recognizing the common functional groups that define reactivity.

concepts.title: Conformational analysis and Newman projections
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Rotation about single bonds, staggered versus eclipsed conformations on Newman projections, and chair conformations of cyclohexane.

concepts.title: Chirality and stereoisomers
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Identifying chiral centers, enantiomers and diastereomers, and assigning R/S configuration with Cahn-Ingold-Prelog priority rules.

concepts.title: Optical activity and Fischer projections
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: How chiral molecules rotate plane-polarized light, racemic mixtures, and representing stereochemistry with Fischer projections.

concepts.title: SN1 and SN2 reactions
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Nucleophilic substitution by concerted backside attack (SN2) versus stepwise carbocation pathway (SN1), and the factors selecting between them.

concepts.title: E1 and E2 elimination reactions
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Elimination to form alkenes by concerted (E2) or stepwise carbocation (E1) mechanisms, including Zaitsev's rule for product selectivity.

concepts.title: Choosing between substitution and elimination
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Using substrate, nucleophile/base strength, and temperature to predict whether SN1, SN2, E1, or E2 dominates.

concepts.title: Electrophilic addition to alkenes
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Addition of electrophiles to C=C double bonds, Markovnikov versus anti-Markovnikov selectivity, and carbocation versus concerted pathways.

concepts.title: Alkyne reactions and synthesis
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Addition reactions of triple bonds, acidity of terminal alkynes, and using acetylide nucleophiles to build carbon chains.

concepts.title: Alcohols: synthesis and reactions
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Preparing alcohols and their conversion to alkyl halides, oxidation states of alcohols, and dehydration to alkenes.

concepts.title: Ethers and epoxide ring-opening
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Forming and cleaving ethers and the regioselective and stereospecific opening of strained epoxide rings under acidic and basic conditions.

concepts.title: Conjugated dienes and the Diels-Alder reaction
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Stability of conjugated dienes and the concerted [4+2] cycloaddition of a diene and dienophile to form a six-membered ring.

concepts.title: Aromaticity and Huckel's rule
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: The cyclic, planar, fully conjugated criteria and Huckel's 4n+2 pi-electron rule that confer special aromatic stability.

concepts.title: Electrophilic aromatic substitution
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Substitution on benzene rings and how existing substituents direct new groups to ortho, meta, or para positions and affect reactivity.

concepts.title: Nucleophilic addition to aldehydes and ketones
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: The polarized carbonyl group and nucleophilic addition reactions (hydrates, acetals, imines, Grignard additions) at the carbonyl carbon.

concepts.title: Nucleophilic acyl substitution
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Interconversion of carboxylic acid derivatives (esters, amides, anhydrides, acid chlorides) by addition-elimination at the carbonyl, ranked by reactivity.

concepts.title: Enols, enolates, and alpha-carbon reactions
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Keto-enol tautomerism, enolate formation, and alpha-carbon reactions such as alkylation and the aldol and Claisen condensations.

concepts.title: Amines: structure, basicity, and reactions
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Naming and classifying amines, their basicity and nucleophilicity, and key reactions including reductive amination and diazonium chemistry.

concepts.title: Infrared (IR) spectroscopy
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Identifying functional groups from characteristic IR absorption bands tied to bond vibrations and stretching frequencies.

concepts.title: Proton NMR spectroscopy
concepts.subject: chemistry
concepts.description: Using chemical shift, integration, and spin-spin splitting in 1H NMR spectra to deduce the structure of an organic molecule.
